title: Kirkin' o' the Tartan
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
April 29, 2018 | "Kirkin' o' the Tartan" | Preacher: Katie Pearson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon fell on the Fifth Sunday of Easter in the Christian calendar, falling within the Eastertide season (the 50 days between Easter and Pentecost). The RCL readings for this date were Acts 11:1-18, Psalm 148, Revelation 21:1-6, and John 13:31-35, focusing on Peter's vision of clean and unclean foods and themes of inclusion and new creation. The "Kirkin' o' the Tartan" (blessing of the tartan) is a Scottish-heritage observance that blends Presbyterian and Scottish traditions with Christian liturgy, typically held around St. Andrew's Day (November 30) but occasionally adapted for spring celebrations of Scottish culture and faith.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in a period of post-General Convention (2015) implementation of resolutions, with ongoing conversations about marriage equality and LGBTQ+ inclusion following formal authorization in 2015. The Diocese of Colorado was led by Bishop Robert O'Neill (serving 2002-2019), and the cathedral was in a stable pastoral period. No major denominational controversies or bishop elections were imminent at this specific time in April 2018.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical late April spring weather with temperatures in the 50s-60s Fahrenheit and preparing for summer season. The city was focused on local municipal elections scheduled for May 2019, though no major crisis or event dominated headlines in the week before April 29. The Capitol Hill neighborhood, home to the cathedral, remained a gentrifying urban center with ongoing development pressures and arts community presence.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Trump administration was in its second year with ongoing Russia investigation developments and Michael Cohen's cooperation with prosecutors becoming public in recent weeks. The Parkland school shooting aftermath dominated ongoing policy debates about gun control. The royal wedding of Prince Harry and Meghan Markle was scheduled for May 19, 2018, receiving significant American media coverage in the weeks preceding. Cannabis legalization debates continued following the 2016 Colorado legalization, with the industry maturing and tax revenue discussions ongoing.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Syrian conflict continued with ongoing Russian involvement and chemical weapons allegations; specifically, the OPCW was investigating alleged chemical attacks in Douma in early April 2018. The Iran nuclear deal (JCPOA) was under threat from the Trump administration's announced intention to withdraw in May 2018, creating diplomatic tension. North Korea was preparing for inter-Korean talks and potential dialogue with the United States after months of escalation.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in spring 2018 was marked by #MeToo movement momentum, with continued reckoning in Hollywood and other institutions. The general mood reflected political polarization and division, with cable news dominating cultural conversation. In Denver specifically, the Rockies baseball season was underway, and sports remained a primary community bonding point after the Broncos' Super Bowl victory in February 2016 remained relatively recent memory.
